extraction.one_line_summary: GUILLEBEAU Philippe won from breakaway after solo attack on final categorized climb with 3km remaining.
tactical_patterns: Trek-Segafredo used Willie SCHMIDT as peloton disruptor on descent before breakaway established, creating chaos benefiting team's break representation.
ROGLIC group allowed breakaway composition to solidify without chase because GC threats were neutralized, revealing Jumbo-Visma comfort with status quo.
CONTI Valerio attacked conservatively on 12%+ climb despite UAE having no breakaway representation, showing team's risk-averse GC defense strategy.
GUILLEBEAU went solo 3km out on steep climb while chasers turned tactical, exploiting psychological hesitation in reduced breakaway group.

rider_signals.SCHMIDT Willie: neutral: BMX-style save on wet descent while disrupting peloton shows bike-handling but ultimately dropped from break he initiated
rider_signals.STANNARD Ian: positive: Hagen repeatedly mentioned as 'revelation' in GC top-10 defense through Basque climbs suggests upgraded status
rider_signals.GUILLEBEAU Philippe: positive: Kicked away from quality climbers on final 12%+ ascent with tactical acumen to attack before tactical stalemate developed
rider_signals.CONTI Valerio: negative: Described as 'cagey' and 'ruthless but easygoing early' on key climb, suggesting overcautious riding cost breakaway position
